Grammar Patterns.jp: AじからBじまで (activity) をします A-ji kara B-ji made (activity) o shimasu
Grammar Patterns.en: I do (activity) from A o'clock to B. [time range + を]

Grammar Patterns.jp: (activity) をしたことがあります (activity) o shita koto ga arimasu
Grammar Patterns.en: I have done (activity) before. [experience: たことがある]

Grammar Patterns.jp: これも (A) の (thing) です kore mo (A) no (thing) desu
Grammar Patterns.en: This is also a (thing) by / from (A). [も + possessive の]

Grammar Patterns.jp: (thing) がどこにでもあります (thing) ga dokonidemo arimasu
(place) にすんでいます (place) ni sunde imasu
Grammar Patterns.en: I live in (place). [住んでいます]

Grammar Patterns.jp: どのくらい (place) にすんでいますか？ dono kurai (place) ni sunde imasu ka?
Grammar Patterns.en: How long have you been living in (place)? [どのくらい + ている question]

Grammar Patterns.jp: (place) のちゅうしんいがいはゆっくりしています (place) no chuushin igai wa yukkuri shite imasu
Grammar Patterns.en: It's relaxed everywhere except the center of (place). [以外 = except]

Grammar Patterns.jp: (A) と (B) はにています (A) to (B) wa nite imasu
Grammar Patterns.en: (A) and (B) are similar. [似ている comparison]

Grammar Patterns.jp: よく (place) にいきますか？ yoku (place) ni ikimasu ka?
Grammar Patterns.en: Do you go to (place) often? [よく + frequency question]

Grammar Patterns.jp: (A) のほうが (B) より (thing) がおおいです (A) no hou ga (B) yori (thing) ga ooi desu
Grammar Patterns.en: (A) has more (thing) than (B). [AのほうがBより comparison]

Grammar Patterns.jp: (A) のほうが (B) よりにんきです (A) no hou ga (B) yori ninki desu

Grammar Patterns.en: I went to / was in (place) for (period). [duration + destination past]

Grammar Patterns.jp: (place) にひっこします (place) ni hikkoshimasu
Grammar Patterns.en: I am moving to (place). [引っ越す]

Grammar Patterns.jp: (thing) がたのしみです (thing) ga tanoshimi desu
Grammar Patterns.en: I am looking forward to (thing). [楽しみです]

Grammar Patterns.jp: (thing) をうえにのせる (thing) o ue ni noseru
Grammar Patterns.en: Put (thing) on top. [上に乗せる placement]

Grammar Patterns.jp: (person) といっしょに (place) にいく (person) to isshoni (place) ni iku
(place) の (thing) はきたないです (place) no (thing) wa kitanai desu
Grammar Patterns.en: The (thing) in (place) is dirty/unclean. [descriptive noun phrase]

Yojijukugo 四字熟語.en: Beauty you can perceive but never grasp; the ephemeral and inexpressible.
A once-in-a-lifetime encounter; treasure the moment because it will never happen in exactly the same way again.
Flowers, birds, wind, and moon; savoring nature and the seasons.
A clear mirror and still water; a calm, undisturbed mind.
Review the old and discover the new.
Clouds and flowing water; living naturally, without clinging.
A hundred flowers in bloom; brilliant variety and many standout talents.
Learn from abroad while keeping a Japanese soul.
Cherry, plum, peach, and apricot; each person blooms in their own way.
